body.title: Update T-vetinari-device: PA2.0 Layer 3 + Alejandro's tasks pipeline
body.result: T-vetinari-device updated with PA2.0 Layer 3 framing (Alejandro pattern) + T-pa20-palace-foundation dependency note; status→sprint
body.description:
T-vetinari-device exists at triage with a vague description ('meta-orchestrator for the whole agent collective'). Two inputs sharpen the spec significantly:

1. PA2.0 identifies Vetinari as the Layer 3 device — the one that models future decisions before they're needed, consuming Layer 1 observations + Layer 2 discovered relationships to pre-stage tasks.

2. Alejandro's coding factory pattern names the missing 'tasks layer': project briefs + Figma/Slack feedback → tasks with implementation details + testing criteria → route to worker tier → automated proof of life → CI pipeline → manual QA. Bottlenecks are humans: PMs (brief→tasks) and manual QA. Vetinari's job is to eliminate the PM bottleneck.

Update T-vetinari-device description to include:
- PA2.0 Layer 3 role: consumes observations (MINION_RESULT, PATTERN_REPORT, OUTCOME_LEARNER_REPORT) to model which decisions will be needed next and pre-stage tasks
- Alejandro's pipeline: brief → tasks(with impl details + test criteria) → proof-of-life → automated review → hold for manual QA
- Implementation shape: VetinariDevice(BaseDevice), receives briefs via comms://vetinari/brief-inbox, decomposes into cc_queue tickets with full description + completion criteria
- Dependency: Layer 1+2 (T-granny-escalation-patterns + T-inference-outcome-learner) should exist before Vetinari can make intelligent routing pre-decisions

**Affected files:** cc_queue ticket T-vetinari-device (description update via cc_queue.py); lab/design_docs/decisions/ stub for this decision
**Design rules:** none (spec update only)
**Scope boundary:** Update T-vetinari-device description and status from triage to sprint. Out of scope: implementing VetinariDevice.
**Completion criteria:** `python3 ${CC_WORKFLOW_TOOLS}/cc_queue.py show T-vetinari-device` shows updated description with PA2.0 framing, Alejandro's pipeline stages, and dependency note; status = sprint
